-{"id":"gt-ngpz","title":"mol-christmas-launch: 3-day execution plan","description":"

---

## Wisp Integration Wave Plan (added 2025-12-21)

Dependency-ordered execution for gt-3x0z (Wisp Molecules) + gt-rana (Patrol System):

### Wave 1 (parallel - no blockers)
- gt-3x0z.1: gt rig init creates .beads-ephemeral/
- gt-3x0z.2: Configure bd for ephemeral molecule bonding
- gt-3x0z.3: gt doctor checks for ephemeral health
- gt-rana.1: Attachment field on pinned beads
- gt-rana.3: mol-deacon-patrol definition

### Wave 2 (after Wave 1)
- gt-3x0z.4: gt spawn --molecule bonds ephemeral (GATE 1)
- gt-rana.2: Daemon attachment detection

### Wave 3-5 (sequential)
- gt-3x0z.5, gt-3x0z.6 → gt-3x0z.7 → gt-3x0z.8 (GATE 2: squash)

### Wave 6+ (patrol integration)
- gt-rana.4: Basic patrol runner (needs gt-rana.3 + gt-3x0z.8)
- gt-3x0z.9: mol-deacon-patrol uses wisp (needs gt-rana.3 + gt-3x0z.8)
- Then: gt-rana.5-7, gt-3x0z.10-12

### Key Gates
1. gt-3x0z.4 - spawn/bond unlocks Phase 2
2. gt-3x0z.8 - squash unlocks patrol integration
3. gt-rana.4 - patrol runner unlocks Phase 2+ patrol

 {"id":"gt-ngu1","title":"Pinned beads should appear first in mail inbox","description":"Currently bd mail inbox sorts by priority then date, but pinned beads (handoff context) should always appear at the top.

**Current behavior:**
- Pinned beads mixed in with regular mail based on priority/date

**Expected behavior:**
- Pinned beads always first in inbox (before priority sorting)
- This enables handoff beads to be the default first item an agent sees

**Implementation:**
1. In mail.go runMailInbox(), after filtering, sort pinned beads to top
2. Within pinned and non-pinned groups, maintain priority/date sort

**Related:**
- gt-r8ej: Implement pinned beads for handoff state
- gt-8h4: Pinned Beads epic

**Acceptance criteria:**
- bd mail inbox shows pinned beads first
- Pinned beads still sorted by priority/date among themselves
- Non-pinned mail sorted normally after pinned section","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:24.133521-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T18:03:04.871622-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T18:03:04.871622-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ngu1","depends_on_id":"gt-8h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:31.978176-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-ni6a","title":"Witness role template + CLAUDE.md","description":"Create the Witness agent's role context:

1. internal/templates/roles/witness.md.tmpl
   - Witness responsibilities (polecat lifecycle)
   - Core loop description
   - Commands available (gt polecats, bd ready, etc)
   - Escalation protocol

2. Generate CLAUDE.md for witness/ directories
   - Context about the rig
   - Mail address: <rig>/witness
   - Startup protocol (gt prime, check inbox)

The Witness is the per-rig 'pit boss' - NOT a global coordinator. It manages polecats for ONE rig only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:18.617496-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:54:13.697998-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:54:13.697998-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ni6a","depends_on_id":"gt-53w6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:37.105264-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

- Polecat → Witness: shutdown requests
- Witness → Deacon: escalations, status
- Crew → Deacon: recycle requests
- Deacon → Mayor: escalations

## Plugins
- .beads/plugins.yaml registry
- Cooldown tracking
- Plugin runner in deacon patrol

 {"id":"gt-rectf","title":"Digest: mol-deacon-patrol","description":"P11","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-26T14:25:17.043804-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T14:25:17.043804-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-26T14:25:17.04375-08:00","close_reason":"Squashed from 9 wisps"}
-{"id":"gt-rgd9x","title":"Deprecate hook files, use pinned beads for propulsion","description":"## Problem

We have TWO mechanisms for 'work on hook':
1. Hook files (.beads/hook-<agent>.json) - explicit state files
2. Pinned beads (status=pinned, assignee=agent) - discoverable via query

This violates Gas Town's 'discovery over explicit state' principle.

## Current State

- Hook files created by gt hook, gt sling, gt handoff <bead>
- Contain SlungWork struct with BeadID, Subject, Context, Args, Formula
- Burned after pickup
- Only work if explicitly created with bead ID argument

Meanwhile:
- Pinned beads already work (Dave's propulsion succeeded)
- But outputAttachmentStatus() excludes Mayor (line 536 in prime.go)
- Handoff mail already carries context

## Proposed Changes

1. **Remove hook file mechanism entirely**
   - Delete internal/wisp/io.go hook read/write functions
   - Remove .beads/hook-*.json file creation

2. **Use pinned beads universally**
   - gt hook <bead> → bd update <bead> --status=pinned --assignee=<self>
   - gt sling <bead> → pin + nudge
   - gt handoff <bead> → pin + mail + restart

3. **Extend discovery to all roles**
   - Fix line 536 in prime.go: remove Crew/Polecat restriction
   - All roles check for pinned beads on startup

4. **Handle Args field**
   - Store in bead description, OR
   - Add args field to bead schema, OR
   - Put in handoff mail body

## Benefits

- Single source of truth (beads)
- Discovery over explicit state
- No file management (create/burn cycle)
- Works across clones automatically (beads sync)

## Files to Modify

- internal/cmd/prime.go (line 536 - extend to all roles)
- internal/cmd/hook.go (use bd update instead of wisp.WriteSlungWork)
- internal/cmd/sling.go (use bd update instead of wisp.WriteSlungWork)
- internal/cmd/handoff.go (use bd update instead of hookBeadForHandoff)
- internal/wisp/ (deprecate hook file functions)

## See Also

- PROPULSION-FIX.md in ~/gt for full analysis","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-26T15:36:31.396028-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T15:53:46.808511-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-26T15:53:46.808511-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented. Hook files deprecated in favor of pinned beads. Commands now use bd update --status=pinned."}
+{"id":"gt-rgd9x","title":"Deprecate hook files, use pinned beads for propulsion","description":"## Problem

Hook files were "deprecated" via comments but NOT eradicated. The code is all still there and actively used.

## Current State (NOT Done)

**Live hook file exists:**
- `/Users/stevey/gt/deacon/.beads/hook-deacon.json`

**Code still calling hook functions:**
- `resume.go:151` - WriteSlungWork
- `park.go:114` - ReadHook  
- `spawn.go:407` - WriteSlungWork
- `molecule_step.go:293` - WriteSlungWork
- `molecule_step.go:368` - BurnHook

**Hook infrastructure still exists:**
- `internal/wisp/io.go` - All hook I/O functions
- `internal/wisp/types.go` - SlungWork, Wisp types, constants
- `.beads/.gitignore` - hook-*.json pattern

**14 docs still reference hooks:**
architecture.md, beads-data-plane.md, molecular-chemistry.md, molecules.md,
pinned-beads-design.md, polecat-lifecycle.md, polecat-wisp-architecture.md,
propulsion-principle.md, session-lifecycle.md, sling-design.md, vision.md,
wisp-architecture.md, witness-patrol-design.md, ~/gt/CLAUDE.md

## Goal: Total Eradication

1. Remove ALL hook file code from callers
2. Delete hook functions from internal/wisp/
3. Delete hook types and constants
4. Remove gitignore patterns
5. Delete any live hook files, convert to pinned beads
6. Update all documentation
7. Verify propulsion cycle works with pinned beads only","status":"pinned","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"gastown/crew/max","created_at":"2025-12-26T15:36:31.396028-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T16:13:49.970042-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-rhfji","title":"gt up: set BD_ACTOR env var when spawning agents","description":"When gt spawns agents (polecats, crew, patrol roles), it should set BD_ACTOR env var so that bd commands (like `bd hook`) know the agent identity.

Currently gt sets GT_ROLE but bd can't check that (no coupling to gt). bd already checks BD_ACTOR, so gt just needs to set it.

Places to update:
- `gt up` for polecats/crew
- Deacon, Witness, Refinery spawning
- Any other agent spawn points

Related: closed bd-fej5 in beads repo as obsolete (fix belongs here).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T12:41:21.092758-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T13:26:47.176904-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T13:26:47.176904-08:00","close_reason":"Closed"}

 {"id":"gt-rixa","title":"Bug: parseLifecycleRequest always matches 'cycle' due to LIFECYCLE prefix","description":"In lifecycle.go, parseLifecycleRequest checks strings.Contains(title, "cycle") first, but the prefix "LIFECYCLE:" contains the word "cycle". This means ALL lifecycle messages match the cycle action, making restart and shutdown unreachable. Fix: check for restart/shutdown before cycle, or use word boundaries.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T16:17:16.083512-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T20:54:19.096667-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T20:54:19.096667-08:00","close_reason":"Orphaned: referenced in commit but not closed"}

-{"id":"gt-zbmg8","title":"gt status should show runtime state, not just structure","description":"## Problem
`gt status` shows structural info (which crews exist) but not runtime reality (which sessions are running).

## Current Output
- Shows 'Crews: [dave emma zoey]' but doesn't indicate dave is crashed
- Missing Deacon from output entirely
- Hooks shown but unclear if agents are alive

## Expected
- Show which tmux sessions are actually running
- Indicate crashed/missing sessions with warning
- Include all roles (Mayor, Deacon, Witnesses, Refineries, Crews)
- Distinguish between:
  - Structure: what COULD run
  - Runtime: what IS running

## Example Better Output
```
Agents
   mayor          ✓ running (gt-mayor)
   deacon         ✓ running (gt-deacon)
   
Rigs
   beads
      witness     ✓ running
      refinery    ✓ running  
      crew/dave   ✗ CRASHED (session missing)
      crew/emma   ✓ running
   gastown
      witness     ✓ running
      refinery    ✓ running
      crew/max    ✗ CRASHED (session missing)
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-26T15:31:12.30459-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T15:31:12.30459-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-zbmg8","title":"gt status should show runtime state, not just structure","description":"## Problem
`gt status` shows structural info (which crews exist) but not runtime reality (which sessions are running).

## Current Output
- Shows 'Crews: [dave emma zoey]' but doesn't indicate dave is crashed
- Missing Deacon from output entirely
- Hooks shown but unclear if agents are alive

## Expected
- Show which tmux sessions are actually running
- Indicate crashed/missing sessions with warning
- Include all roles (Mayor, Deacon, Witnesses, Refineries, Crews)
- Distinguish between:
  - Structure: what COULD run
  - Runtime: what IS running

## Example Better Output
```
Agents
   mayor          ✓ running (gt-mayor)
   deacon         ✓ running (gt-deacon)
   
Rigs
   beads
      witness     ✓ running
      refinery    ✓ running  
      crew/dave   ✗ CRASHED (session missing)
      crew/emma   ✓ running
   gastown
      witness     ✓ running
      refinery    ✓ running
      crew/max    ✗ CRASHED (session missing)
```","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-26T15:31:12.30459-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T16:12:51.206283-08:00"}
